name: Russia
headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — FIFTH consecutive day of major drone assault. Overnight May 17-18: 524 attack drones + 22 missiles — LARGEST single-night attack package of the May arc (vs. 287 drones May 17, 294 May 16). Ukrainian air defenses neutralized 503 drones + 4 missiles (96% intercept rate). NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: Russian drone struck Chinese-owned cargo ship KSL Deyang (Chinese crew, Marshall Islands flag) in Black Sea approaching Ukrainian port — first documented third-country-vessel strike in the current arc. Strike occurred 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival for Xi summit. Cumulative 5-day window (May 14-18): 2,000+ drones + 78 missiles in 120 hours post-ceasefire-expiry. Pattern transitions from policy-arc (4 days, May 17 canonical) to entrenched-policy (5 days). Already at floor (0.0); no downward movement available. Documentation expansion: new sub-category 'third-country-vessel-strike-during-declared-targeting' anchored as Tier-4 BND-dimension methodology category.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. Five consecutive days of 200-524 drone assault confirms awareness of civilian-targeting and third-country-vessel-strike-risk is present and disregarded as policy.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. Cumulative 5-day toll across Ukraine; KSL Deyang crew casualty risk imposed on third-country nationals.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC2 proportionality: 524-drone single-night package is the largest of the arc; AC5 follow-through: pattern extended into 5th day despite international condemnation and 96% interception rate.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL on civilian protection violated; third-country-vessel-strike adds new IHL category (Article 51, Additional Protocol I — prohibition on indiscriminate attacks affecting non-belligerent parties). Marshall Islands flag of KSL Deyang triggers maritime IHL frame.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No accountability for KSL Deyang strike; no investigation; no notification to flag state (Marshall Islands) or ship-owner state (China).

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. 5-day attack arc consolidates systems-level pattern of post-ceasefire bombardment-as-policy. The third-country-vessel-strike adds a new systems-failure dimension: target-discrimination protocols either absent or knowingly overridden.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. I1 consistency-under-pressure: striking a Chinese-owned vessel 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival for Xi summit demonstrates either (a) target-discrimination breakdown indicating systemic disregard, or (b) deliberate signal — either reading consistent with floor INT. The selective-IHL-compliance pattern (May 16 528-body remains-return paired with concurrent civilian bombardment) extends as canonical integrity-inconsistency signal.

evidence.summary: NPR / Al Jazeera May 18: Russia launched 524 attack drones + 22 missiles overnight May 17-18; Ukrainian air defenses neutralized 503 drones + 4 missiles (96% intercept rate). Largest single-night attack package of the May arc.

Al Jazeera May 18: Russian drone struck Chinese-owned cargo ship KSL Deyang (Chinese crew, Marshall Islands flag) in Black Sea approaching Ukrainian port. First documented third-country-vessel-strike in the current arc.

Diplomat / Al Jazeera: KSL Deyang strike occurred 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival for Xi summit. Geopolitical context: Xi publicly reassured Putin of continued support; China supplying 90% of Russia's dual-use drone technology.
Kyiv Independent: corroboration of multi-oblast strike package and casualty figures for May 18 attack.

keyEvidence: Overnight May 17-18: 524 attack drones + 22 missiles — LARGEST single-night attack package of the May arc.
FIFTH consecutive day of major drone assault (May 14-18: 2,000+ drones + 78 missiles in 120 hours).
Pattern transitions from policy-arc (4 days) to entrenched-policy (5 days).
NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: Russian drone struck Chinese-owned cargo ship KSL Deyang (third-country-vessel-strike).
Timing: 24-48 hours before Putin's Beijing arrival for Xi summit.
Floor sustained at 0.0; no downward movement available.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The fifth consecutive day of major drone attacks extends the documented pattern from policy-arc (4 days, May 17 canonical) to entrenched-policy (5 days). The 524-drone single-night package is the largest of the arc. The KSL Deyang third-country-vessel strike anchors a new methodology category. Already at floor — no downward movement available. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Continuation of attack pattern into May 19+ (6-day arc test)
China response to KSL Deyang strike (silence vs. protest vs. summit-reframing)
Marshall Islands flag-state response (raised in Vanuatu UNGA vote context)
Putin-Xi summit joint statement May 19-20 — China-Russia war-support formalization signal
Ukrainian retaliatory escalation pattern (oil refinery strike May 18)
ICC engagement on post-ceasefire civilian targeting
carryForward: Post-ceasefire offensive-surge phase (now 5-day arc; entrenched policy)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology candidate: 'third-country-vessel-strike-during-declared-targeting' — a Tier-4 BND-dimension sub-anchor under indiscriminate-attack scoring. Applies when a belligerent's targeting affects non-belligerent third-country vessels (commercial or otherwise). Distinguished from accidental-strike by (a) intelligence access to vessel identification systems, (b) repeated pattern within the arc, and (c) timing relative to diplomatic signaling. Russia-KSL Deyang May 18 is the canonical instance. Cross-references: maritime IHL (Geneva Convention II, San Remo Manual on Armed Conflicts at Sea).
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Floor confirmation; no score change.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
